task_completion.description: Open the target document and the formatting guide.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A word processor (e.g., Calligra Words or LibreOffice Writer) is open displaying 'the_bear_script.odt', and a text editor or viewer is open displaying 'script_formatting_guide.txt'.

task_completion.description: Format the title, subtitle, and author.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'THE BEAR', 'A Joke in One Act', and 'by Anton Chekhov' are visible at the top of the document and have been formatted distinctly (e.g., centered, different font sizes, or separated from the main text) according to standard title page conventions.
The heading 'DRAMATIS PERSONAE' is visible and formatted as a heading, followed by the character names POPOVA, SMIRNOV, and LUKA.

task_completion.description: Apply bold formatting to character names in the dialogue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the main script body, the character names (POPOVA, SMIRNOV, LUKA) that precede their spoken lines are visibly formatted in bold text.

task_completion.description: Apply italic formatting to stage directions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Parenthetical stage directions such as '(weeping)', '(aside)', '(enters)', or '(stamps his foot)' are visibly formatted in italic text, while the surrounding spoken dialogue remains in normal text.

The word processor shows no unsaved changes indicator (e.g., no asterisk next to the filename in the title bar), or there is a visible save action/notification confirming the file was saved to '/home/ga/Documents/the_bear_script.odt'.

integrity.description: The agent used the word processor's graphical interface to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Formatting is applied via the word processor's UI (e.g., selecting text and clicking Bold/Italic buttons), rather than using a terminal script to modify the underlying ODT XML files.

integrity.description: Rich text formatting was actually applied, not just markdown symbols.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text appears genuinely bolded or italicized in the word processor, and the Bold/Italic toolbar icons are highlighted when the text is selected. The agent did not simply type asterisks (e.g., **POPOVA**) around the words.

integrity.description: The original script content was preserved during formatting.
integrity.visual_evidence: The dialogue text (e.g., 'I shall remain faithful to the grave', 'I invite you to a duel') remains intact and was not accidentally deleted, replaced, or corrupted while applying the formatting.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The document to format is '/home/ga/Documents/the_bear_script.odt'. The expected title is 'THE BEAR', subtitle 'A Joke in One Act', and author 'by Anton Chekhov'. The character list heading is 'DRAMATIS PERSONAE' with characters POPOVA, SMIRNOV, LUKA. Character names in dialogue must be bolded. Stage directions in parentheses (e.g., '(weeping)', '(aside)', '(enters)', '(jumps up)', '(stamps his foot)') must be italicized. Normal dialogue text (e.g., 'I shall remain faithful', 'I invite you to a duel') must NOT be bolded or italicized.
